diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute hepatocellular injury / autoimmune hepatitis flare
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilirubinuria from conjugated hyperbilirubinaemia — an objective marker of hepatobiliary dysfunction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Jaundice (yellowing of eyes and skin), fluctuating

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Visible jaundice indicates significant hyperbilirubinaemia and acute liver injury; fluctuating jaundice is characteristic of AIH.
Profound fatigue is a dominant symptom of active autoimmune hepatitis, though non-specific in isolation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Migratory polyarthralgia of the small joints of the hands and wrists

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Migratory small-joint arthralgia is a recognised extrahepatic autoimmune feature accompanying AIH.
Amenorrhoea/oligomenorrhoea is associated with active autoimmune hepatitis; weak and non-specific but part of the constellation.
Cutaneous involvement can accompany autoimmune/immune-mediated disease; non-specific supporting feature.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known type 1 diabetes mellitus (co-occurring autoimmune disease)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A pre-existing organ-specific autoimmune disease raises the prior probability of a second autoimmune condition such as AIH.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hepatomegaly with a firm, tender liver edge palpable 3 cm below the costal margin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A tender, enlarged liver indicates acute hepatic inflammation — a clinician palpation finding the AI Doctor cannot obtain remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Alert and oriented with no asterixis (no clinical encephalopathy)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of encephalopathy and asterixis argues against current fulminant failure and supports urgent (not emergency) triage; formal assessment for asterixis is a clinician examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hepatocellular pattern liver function tests with raised bilirubin

investigation_details.investigation_name: Liver function tests (transaminases, bilirubin, ALP, albumin)
investigation_details.expected_result: Raised transaminases with elevated bilirubin (hepatocellular pattern) — pending venous bloods
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Severity of hepatocellular injury and cholestasis unquantified; cannot stratify risk or track a flare.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A hepatocellular LFT pattern is required to characterise the injury; obtainable only via bloods the AI cannot draw.

symptoms.symptom_name: Elevated serum IgG / positive autoantibodies (ANA, ASMA, anti-LKM-1)

investigation_details.investigation_name: Immunoglobulins (IgG) and autoimmune liver serology (ANA, ASMA, anti-LKM-1)
investigation_details.expected_result: Elevated IgG with positive ANA and/or ASMA — presumed pending serology
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Autoimmune aetiology cannot be supported; diagnosis and immunosuppression decisions cannot proceed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Elevated IgG with characteristic autoantibodies is central to the autoimmune-hepatitis diagnostic score; only obtainable via serology.

investigation_details.expected_result: Pending — a prolonged INR would indicate synthetic failure and acute liver failure
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Synthetic failure (the defining feature of acute liver failure) would go undetected.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: INR/coagulation is the key test to detect synthetic failure; it defines whether the flare has become emergency-level.

investigation_details.expected_result: Expected negative — required to exclude viral hepatitis before attributing to autoimmune cause
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: A treatable/transmissible viral hepatitis could be missed or mislabelled as autoimmune.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Viral causes must be excluded serologically; they cannot be ruled out on history alone.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Fluctuating jaundice and fatigue, co-occurring autoimmune disease (type 1 diabetes), extrahepatic arthralgia and (presumed) elevated IgG with positive autoantibodies together form the characteristic autoimmune-hepatitis picture.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Acute liver failure red-flag screen (currently negative)
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Encephalopathy (asterixis/confusion) and coagulopathy define progression to acute liver failure; both are absent/pending here, which sets the tier at urgent rather than emergency but must be actively re-screened.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Characterise and quantify the hepatocellular injury same-day.
Detect synthetic failure — the test that distinguishes an urgent flare from an emergency.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immunoglobulins (IgG) and autoimmune liver serology (ANA, ASMA, anti-LKM-1)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Support the autoimmune aetiology and inform specialist management.
Exclude viral hepatitis before attributing to an autoimmune cause.
Baseline haematology and renal function; assess for cytopenias and hydration/renal status.
Assess liver parenchyma and biliary tree and exclude biliary obstruction; arranged through the assessing service, not the AI.
Definitive for autoimmune hepatitis but a specialist-directed procedure — never ordered or performed via telehealth.
